summary: Cipher backend iteration 3 — WebRTC signaling endpoints (/api/calls/signal POST, /api/calls/signals GET). 9/9 new pytest tests PASSED on the public URL, and all 31 regression tests from iterations 1 & 2 continue to pass (40/40 total). All review spec items are covered: signal POST/retrieve round-trip for offer/answer/candidate/hangup, embedded from_user {id, display_name, username}, per-recipient scoping (A→B NOT visible to C), FIFO created_at ordering, delete-after-read semantics, 404 for non-existent recipient, and 401 for unauthenticated POST/GET.

minor.issue: `type` accepts any string (offer|answer|candidate|hangup|ringing are only in a code comment). Consider Literal[...] / enum validation to reject arbitrary types like 'pwn' which currently get stored.
No rate limit / size check on `payload` dict. A malicious client could POST very large SDP/candidate payloads and fill the signals collection (mitigated only by 3600s TTL).
Self-signaling is allowed (to_user_id == caller). Worth rejecting with 400 to prevent loopback noise.

Read-then-delete is not atomic (find → delete_many). Two concurrent GETs from the same user could each return the same batch before either delete lands. Consider find_one_and_delete loop or transactional delete on matched ids. Low risk in practice because the same user typically only polls from one browser tab.
Hard cap of .to_list(100) with no pagination — burst of >100 ICE candidates in a single tick would leave the overflow in DB until next poll. Reasonable for MVP.
`call_id` is free-form client-supplied and never validated as UUID — a client can collide call_ids across peers. Minor, but consider enforcing uuid format.

action_items: OPTIONAL: tighten CallSignalIn.type to Literal['offer','answer','candidate','hangup','ringing'] to reject bad types at pydantic layer.
OPTIONAL: enforce payload size limit (e.g., < 64KB) on /calls/signal.
OPTIONAL: make read-and-delete in /calls/signals atomic using find_one_and_delete in a loop or a session/transaction to avoid duplicate delivery under concurrent polling.
OPTIONAL: add long-polling or WebSocket so clients don't hammer /calls/signals — current REST polling design will have ~1-2s signaling latency minimum.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 793 lines — crossed the 700-line threshold flagged in iterations 1 & 2. Strongly recommend splitting into routers/ (auth, guest, servers, emojis, dms, files, calls) before iteration 4.
Signaling correctness: from_user embed, to_user_id scoping filter, _id projection, ISO string created_at and TTL index (3600s) on signals.created_at — all verified.
Index on signals.to_user_id is created in startup; good for the hot-path poll query.
Delete-after-read semantics behave correctly (verified: subsequent GET returns empty for the consumed batch).
404 is returned for unknown to_user_id before the insert, so orphan signals cannot accumulate (verified).
No XSS/escape concern on payload because payload is JSON dict passed through verbatim (SDP/candidate strings are opaque to the server).
test_credentials: Admin: admin@cipher.io / Admin@Cipher2026 (not used here). Tests register their own TEST_call* users via /api/auth/register.
seed_data_creation: Tests create ~14 TEST_call* users (two per round-trip test plus three for the scoping test). Signals are auto-pruned by the 3600s TTL and by the read-delete behavior of the endpoint itself.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v` — 40 tests, ~108s. New signaling tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_calls_signaling.py. The signaling transport is REST polling (no WebSocket), so any frontend iteration must poll /api/calls/signals. Signals are auto-deleted on read — two simultaneous GETs from the same user will race. If a 'ringing' event or WebSocket upgrade is introduced next, extend this file. TTL = 3600s on signals.created_at.
rca of the issue: N/A — no backend bugs. All 9 new endpoint tests and all 31 regression tests pass against the public URL https://team-secure.preview.emergentagent.com/api.
